site.br
Some Anecdotes
projects.iga.title: site.hopen
A Shopping Expedition; a Systems Failure — No Fire
site.hclose
projects.iga.1: Broken systems grate on me, but never so much as when they eat into my time (my most valuable asset), or when they otherwise intrude on my existence.
projects.iga.2: Some years ago, I stopped at my local IGA supermarket to acquire the usual bread, milk and other sundries. The queue was, seemingly, unprecedented,
as it made its serpentine path through the shop. I'd not long moved to the suburb and was not overly familiar with the shop nor the staff. I watched
for a minute and realised that they had two young girls on the checkouts; one had not completed her RSA course and was prohibited from serving customers
wanting to purchase alcoholic beverages; the other was driving a register that had a malfunctioning eftpos terminal and was, consequently, unable to
projects.iga.3: From across the shop I called out to the apparently more experienced of the two, "Oi, she's working half speed because of RSA, and you're working
less than half speed because of the EFTPOS issue. It would make more sense for your mate to work your til, and for you to run at full speed on your
mate's til. Where's your manager???". A short conversation ensued, then they just swapped places — just like that. I wasn't rude at all but
the other customers were all looking at me as though I'd just taken a piss on the floor. The line began to move quickly though, following that minor
remediation. Their manager ought to have recognised the issue and addressed it. It seems that many people either fail to perceive systemic problems,
or are reluctant to intervene where there is a perceived lack of mandate or authority.
